_source: NVIDIA Error Code Reference for Xid Messages (public docs)
_notes: Curated subset of the most common Xid codes encountered on consumer + datacenter NVIDIA GPUs. Severity : info | warn | fail. Maintained MIT, redistribution OK.

13.name: Graphics Engine Exception
13.cause: Driver mismatch, application bug, or hardware fault — most often a driver/library version skew
13.remediation: Verify driver + CUDA toolkit versions match. Reinstall driver if recent. If recurring on one app, file an upstream bug.

31.name: GPU memory page fault
31.cause: Application read/write outside allocated VRAM — could be OOM-adjacent or a CUDA kernel bug
31.remediation: Check if VRAM was nearly full at the time. Reduce batch size. If app-specific, report to its maintainers.

32.name: Invalid or corrupted push buffer stream
32.cause: PCIe transmission errors or driver state corruption
32.remediation: Reseat the GPU. Check PCIe slot + power connectors. Try a different PCIe slot.

43.name: GPU stopped processing
43.cause: Application explicitly aborted a CUDA stream or kernel hit a guard
43.remediation: Usually benign — application caught a runtime error and bailed. Look at the app's stderr.

45.name: Preemptive cleanup, due to previous errors -- Most likely to see when running multiple cuda applications
45.cause: Cascade after a previous Xid (e.g. OOM, kernel timeout)
45.remediation: Treat as a follow-up to the actual root cause Xid that preceded it.

61.name: GPU Compute Engine timeout (hang)
61.cause: A CUDA kernel ran past its watchdog deadline (often : infinite loop, deadlock, or huge unsegmented batch)
61.remediation: Reduce batch size. Check for kernel deadlocks. Disable TDR if you really need long-running kernels (server-class).

62.name: GPU Internal Micro-controller Halt
62.cause: GSP / falcon micro-controller error — usually transient, driver attempts to reset
62.remediation: If happens once : ignore. Recurring : try disabling GSP firmware via nvidia.NVreg_EnableGpuFirmware=0 module param.

63.name: ECC page retirement / row remapping
63.cause: A VRAM cell was retired automatically (datacenter cards : A100, H100, etc.)
63.remediation: Normal on aging datacenter cards. Check /api/ecc-health for remapped row count. If >100 → schedule replacement.

64.name: ECC page retirement recording failure
64.cause: Driver failed to persist the retirement metadata
64.remediation: Memory hardware is degrading. Schedule replacement.

68.name: Video processor exception
68.cause: NVENC/NVDEC engine error — common with corrupted video files
68.remediation: Usually app-specific. Check stderr of the encode/decode app.

74.name: NVLINK error
74.cause: NVLink fabric / cable / slot fault (datacenter only)
74.remediation: Reseat NVLink bridge. Check link status via nvidia-smi nvlink -s.

79.name: GPU has fallen off the bus
79.cause: PSU power dropout, PCIe reset, or fatal hardware failure
79.remediation: Reseat power cables. Check PSU rail voltages. If recurring : test card in another rig before RMA.

92.name: High single-bit ECC error rate
92.remediation: Monitor /api/ecc-health uncorrected counter. If it climbs, schedule replacement.

94.name: Contained ECC error
94.cause: Double-bit ECC error in VRAM, contained by the driver (no data loss to the kernel)
94.remediation: Datacenter cards : check /api/ecc-health. Consumer cards : reduce overclock / GPU voltage.

95.name: Uncontained ECC error
95.cause: Memory error the driver couldn't recover from — process killed
95.remediation: If recurring : memory is failing. Reduce mem-clock offset. Consider replacement.

109.name: GPU monitoring contended access
109.cause: Two tools polling NVML aggressively at the same time (nvidia-smi vs DCGM vs your dashboard)
109.remediation: Increase sampler interval. Disable redundant monitoring tools.

119.name: GSP RPC Timeout
119.cause: GPU System Processor (GSP) firmware became unresponsive — typical on driver 535+ with consumer cards
119.remediation: Disable GSP via nvidia.NVreg_EnableGpuFirmware=0 in kernel module options. Reload driver.
